zotero.abstractNote: We study the identifiability of causal models with location-scale noise, i.e., models of the form Y = f(X) + g(X)E, where E is independent of X. Such models are widely used in practice, for instance, to account for heteroscedasticity. We show that if the noise E is non-Gaussian, the causal direction is identifiable from observational data alone, provided that the functions f and g are sufficiently flexible. We further propose a practical estimation procedure based on neural networks and demonstrate its effectiveness on synthetic and real-world data.
